                best_gpu = max(valid_gpus, key=lambda g: GPU_MEM_SIZE - (gpu_sizes[g] + size))
            else:  # first_fit
                best_gpu = valid_gpus[0]
            
            placement[best_gpu].append(model)
            gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
            gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Place in order: large, medium, small
    place_models(large_indices, "best_fit")
    place_models(medium_indices, "best_fit")
    place_models(small_indices, "most_space")
    
    # Local search: iterative improvement with enhanced swap logic
    max_iterations = 3000
    improvement_threshold = 5e-8  # Slightly stricter threshold
    
    for iteration in range(max_iterations):
        # Find GPU with maximum KVPR
        kvprs = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                kvprs[g] = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
        
        if torch.all(kvprs == 0):
            break
        
        max_kvpr = torch.max(kvprs).item()
        max_gpu = torch.argmax(kvprs).item()
        
        if not placement[max_gpu]:
            break
        
        moved = False
        
        # Try moving models from max_gpu
        models_on_max = placement[max_gpu]
        
        # Sort by pressure/size ratio (more aggressive)
        model_impacts = []
        for model in models_on_max:
            idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressures[idx].item()
            s = model_sizes[idx].item()
            ratio = p / (s + 1e-9)
            model_impacts.append((ratio, model, p, s))
        
        model_impacts.sort(reverse=True)
        
        for _, model, p, s in model_impacts:
            # Try moving to other GPUs
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Calculate new KVPRs
                new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                if new_max_size <= 0:
                    continue
                
                new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                
                # Accept if improvement
                if new_overall_max < max_kvpr - improvement_threshold:
                    placement[max_gpu].remove(model)
                    placement[target_gpu].append(model)
                    gpu_sizes[max_gpu] -= s
                    gpu_sizes[target_gpu] += s
                    gpu_pressures[max_gpu] -= p
                    gpu_pressures[target_gpu] += p
                    moved = True
                    break
            
            if moved:
                break
        
        if not moved:
            # Try swapping between max_gpu and another GPU
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu or not placement[target_gpu]:
                    continue
                
                # Try swapping models
                for src_model in placement[max_gpu]:
                    src_idx = models.index(src_model)
                    src_p = model_pressures[src_idx].item()
                    src_s = model_sizes[src_idx].item()
                    
                    for tgt_model in placement[target_gpu]:
                        tgt_idx = models.index(tgt_model)
                        tgt_p = model_pressures[tgt_idx].item()
                        tgt_s = model_sizes[tgt_idx].item()
                        
                        # Check if swap is valid
                        if (gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE or
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE):
                            continue
                        
                        # Calculate new KVPRs
                        new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                        new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                        new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                        new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                        
                        new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                        new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                        new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                        
                        # Accept if significant improvement
                        if new_overall_max < max_kvpr - improvement_threshold:
                            # Perform swap
                            placement[max_gpu].remove(src_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].remove(tgt_model)
                            placement[max_gpu].append(tgt_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].append(src_model)
                            
                            gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                            gpu_pressures[max_gpu] = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                            gpu_pressures[target_gpu] = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                            
                            moved = True
                            break
                    
                    if moved:
                        break
                
                if moved:
                    break
        
        if not moved:
            break
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != n_models:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {n_models} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import torch
import math
from typing import List, Dict

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    n_models = len(models)
    if n_models == 0:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Extract model properties
    model_sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    model_pressures = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Validate total memory constraint
    total_size = model_sizes.sum().item()
    total_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    if total_size > total_memory:
        raise ValueError(f"Total model size {total_size}GB exceeds total GPU memory {total_memory}GB")
    
    # VARIANT: Enhanced Priority-Based Packing with Adaptive Best-Fit and Greedy Post-Optimization
    # Key changes:
    # - Use adaptive pressure-to-size ratio with dynamic scaling
    # - Replace fixed tie-breaker with entropy-based free space preference
    # - Reduce refinement iterations but increase swap depth
    # - Add early termination based on KVPR stability
    # - Improve edge case handling for single-model GPUs
    
    # Step 1: Prioritize models using adaptive pressure/size ratio
    # Apply logarithmic scaling to reduce dominance of very large models
    size_log = torch.log1p(model_sizes)  # log(1 + size) to dampen large sizes
    pressure_to_size = model_pressures / (size_log + 1e-8)
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(pressure_to_size, descending=True)
    
    # Step 2: Initial Assignment with Enhanced Best-Fit
    placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    gpu_pressures = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        model = models[idx]
        size = model_sizes[idx].item()
        pressure = model_pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Find valid GPUs
        valid_mask = gpu_sizes + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE
        if not valid_mask.any():
            raise ValueError(f"Cannot place model {model.model_name} due to memory overflow")
        
        valid_indices = torch.where(valid_mask)[0]
        
        # Calculate resulting KVPR for each valid GPU
        new_sizes = gpu_sizes[valid_indices] + size
        new_pressures = gpu_pressures[valid_indices] + pressure
        kvprs = new_pressures / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_sizes)
        
        # Adaptive tie-breaker: prefer GPUs with higher entropy in free space distribution
        # (promotes balanced packing)
        free_spaces = GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_sizes
        free_entropy = -torch.sum(free_spaces * torch.log(free_spaces + 1e-8)) / len(free_spaces)
        scores = kvprs - (0.1 * free_entropy)  # Minimize KVPR, maximize entropy (diversity)
        
        best_idx = torch.argmin(scores).item()
        best_gpu = valid_indices[best_idx].item()
        
        placement[best_gpu].append(model)
        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
        gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Step 3: Greedy Refinement with Deep Swap Search and Early Termination
    max_iterations = 500  # Reduced for speed, but deeper search per iteration
    improvement_threshold = 1e-8
    stability_window = 5  # Early termination if no improvement in last 5 iterations
    last_improvement = 0
    
    for iteration in range(max_iterations):
        # Calculate current KVPRs
        kvprs = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                kvprs[g] = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
        
        if torch.all(kvprs == 0):
            break
        
        max_kvpr = torch.max(kvprs).item()
        max_gpu = torch.argmax(kvprs).item()
        
        if not placement[max_gpu]:
            break
        
        moved = False
        
        # Strategy A: Aggressive model reassignment from high-KVPR GPU
        # Sort by impact: higher pressure * size ratio gets priority
        models_on_max = placement[max_gpu]
        model_impacts = []
        for model in models_on_max:
            idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressures[idx].item()
            s = model_sizes[idx].item()
            impact = p * (1.0 + 0.7 * (s / 20.0))  # Slightly higher weight on size
            model_impacts.append((impact, model, p, s))
        
        model_impacts.sort(reverse=True)
        
        for _, model, p, s in model_impacts:
            # Try to move to any other GPU with memory space
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Calculate potential new KVPRs
                new_max_p = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_s = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_p = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_s = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                # Avoid creating empty GPUs unless forced
                if new_max_s <= 0 and len(placement[max_gpu]) == 1:
                    continue
                
                new_max_kvpr = new_max_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_s) if new_max_s < GPU_MEM_SIZE else float('inf')
                new_target_kvpr = new_target_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_s)
                
                # Track max KVPR across all GPUs
                other_max = 0.0
                for g in range(gpu_num):
                    if g != max_gpu and g != target_gpu and gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                        kvpr = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
                        if kvpr > other_max:
                            other_max = kvpr
                
                new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr, other_max)
                
                if new_overall_max < max_kvpr - improvement_threshold:
                    # Apply move
                    placement[max_gpu].remove(model)
                    placement[target_gpu].append(model)
                    gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = new_max_s
                    gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = new_target_s
                    gpu_pressures[max_gpu] = new_max_p
                    gpu_pressures[target_gpu] = new_target_p
                    moved = True
                    last_improvement = iteration
                    break
            
            if moved:
                break
        
        # Strategy B: Multi-level swap search with depth limit
        if not moved:
            # Only perform swaps if we still have room for improvement
            # Explore swaps involving models from max_gpu and other GPUs
            # Prioritize swaps where the model being moved has high pressure and is large
            for src_model in sorted(placement[max_gpu], key=lambda m: m.req_rate / m.slo, reverse=True):
                src_idx = models.index(src_model)
                src_p = model_pressures[src_idx].item()
                src_s = model_sizes[src_idx].item()
                
                # Try swapping with models from other GPUs (sorted by low pressure to avoid breaking balance)
                for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                    if target_gpu == max_gpu or not placement[target_gpu]:
                        continue
                    
                    # Sort target models by increasing pressure to avoid removing high-impact models
                    for tgt_model in sorted(placement[target_gpu], key=lambda m: m.req_rate / m.slo):
                        tgt_idx = models.index(tgt_model)
                        tgt_p = model_pressures[tgt_idx].item()
                        tgt_s = model_sizes[tgt_idx].item()
                        
                        # Check memory constraints
                        if (gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE or
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE):
                            continue
                        
                        # Calculate new KVPRs
                        new_max_p = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                        new_max_s = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                        new_target_p = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                        new_target_s = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                        
                        if new_max_s <= 0 or new_target_s <= 0:
                            continue
                        
                        new_max_kvpr = new_max_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_s)
                        new_target_kvpr = new_target_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_s)
                        
                        other_max = 0.0
                        for g in range(gpu_num):
                            if g != max_gpu and g != target_gpu and gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                                kvpr = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
                                if kvpr > other_max:
                                    other_max = kvpr
                        
                        new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr, other_max)
                        
                        if new_overall_max < max_kvpr - improvement_threshold:
                            # Apply swap
                            placement[max_gpu].remove(src_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].remove(tgt_model)
                            placement[max_gpu].append(tgt_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].append(src_model)
                            
                            gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = new_max_s
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = new_target_s
                            gpu_pressures[max_gpu] = new_max_p
                            gpu_pressures[target_gpu] = new_target_p
                            
                            moved = True
                            last_improvement = iteration
                            break
                    
                    if moved:
                        break
                
                if moved:
                    break
        
        # Early termination: if no improvement in last few iterations
        if not moved and (iteration - last_improvement) >= stability_window:
            break
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != n_models:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {n_models} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import torch
import math
from typing import List, Dict

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    n_models = len(models)
    if n_models == 0:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Extract model properties
    model_sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    model_pressures = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Validate total memory constraint
    total_size = model_sizes.sum().item()
    total_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    if total_size > total_memory:
        raise ValueError(f"Total model size {total_size}GB exceeds total GPU memory {total_memory}GB")
    
    # Calculate theoretical lower bound on max KVPR
    total_pressure = model_pressures.sum().item()
    min_kvpr_lower_bound = total_pressure / (total_memory - total_size)
    
    # VARIANT: Modified Weighted Round Robin with Adaptive Greedy Refinement
    # Changes:
    # - Use dynamic efficiency threshold based on model size
    # - Reduce swap iteration count but increase per-iteration exploration
    # - Add early termination based on stability
    # - Use a more aggressive impact scoring with memory penalty
    # - Improve edge case handling for small models

    # Step 1: Sort models by pressure/size ratio (descending), with size-based smoothing
    # Smaller models get slightly higher priority to improve packing
    size_normalized_efficiency = model_pressures / (model_sizes + 1e-9)
    # Apply size-dependent bias: favor smaller models in high-efficiency region
    size_bias = torch.exp(-model_sizes / 25.0)  # Smaller models get boosted
    adjusted_efficiency = size_normalized_efficiency * (1.0 + 0.3 * size_bias)
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(adjusted_efficiency, descending=True)
    
    # Step 2: Weighted Round Robin Assignment with dynamic load balancing
    placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    gpu_pressures = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Use a more sophisticated assignment policy: prefer GPUs with low KVPR and high free space
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        model = models[idx]
        size = model_sizes[idx].item()
        pressure = model_pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Find valid GPUs
        valid_gpus = [g for g in range(gpu_num) if gpu_sizes[g] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE]
        
        if not valid_gpus:
            # Emergency: find GPU with most free space, even if it's tight
            free_spaces = [GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g].item() for g in range(gpu_num)]
            best_gpu = torch.argmax(torch.tensor(free_spaces)).item()
            if gpu_sizes[best_gpu] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                raise ValueError(f"Cannot place model {model.model_name} due to memory overflow")
            valid_gpus = [best_gpu]
        
        # Choose GPU based on minimizing the new max KVPR, with a small bias toward memory capacity
        best_gpu = None
        best_max_kvpr = float('inf')
        best_score = float('inf')
        
        for gpu_id in valid_gpus:
            new_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] + pressure
            new_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size
            new_kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
            
            # Estimate overall max KVPR after this assignment
            temp_max_kvpr = new_kvpr
            for g in range(gpu_num):
                if g != gpu_id and gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                    other_kvpr = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
                    if other_kvpr > temp_max_kvpr:
                        temp_max_kvpr = other_kvpr
            
            # Add a penalty for low free space to avoid future blocking
            free_space = GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size
            score = temp_max_kvpr + 0.1 * (1.0 / (free_space + 1e-9))  # Penalize tight slots
            
            if score < best_score:
                best_score = score
                best_max_kvpr = temp_max_kvpr
                best_gpu = gpu_id
        
        placement[best_gpu].append(model)
        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
        gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Step 3: Enhanced Greedy Refinement with Adaptive Strategy
    # Reduced iterations but smarter exploration:
    # - Focus on high-impact swaps
    # - Use a decaying improvement threshold
    # - Add a stabilization check

    max_iterations = 1000  # Reduced from 2000 for faster convergence
    improvement_threshold = 1e-8  # Slightly looser threshold
    decay_rate = 0.98  # Gradually reduce threshold
    stability_check = 0  # Track consecutive non-improving iterations
    max_stability = 100  # Early termination if no improvement
    
    for iteration in range(max_iterations):
        # Calculate current KVPRs
        kvprs = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                kvprs[g] = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
        
        if torch.all(kvprs == 0):
            break
        
        max_kvpr = torch.max(kvprs).item()
        max_gpu = torch.argmax(kvprs).item()
        
        if not placement[max_gpu]:
            break
        
        moved = False
        current_threshold = improvement_threshold * (decay_rate ** iteration)
        
        # Try moving models from max_gpu to other GPUs
        models_on_max = placement[max_gpu]
        model_impacts = []
        
        for model in models_on_max:
            idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressures[idx].item()
            s = model_sizes[idx].item()
            # Impact = pressure × (1 + size penalty), with memory penalty for large models
            impact = p * (1.0 + 0.6 * (s / 20.0) * (1.0 if s > 15 else 0.5))
            model_impacts.append((impact, model, p, s))
        
        model_impacts.sort(reverse=True)
        
        for _, model, p, s in model_impacts:
            # Try to move to any other GPU with space
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Calculate new KVPRs
                new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                if new_max_size <= 0:
                    continue
                
                new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                
                # Accept if improvement exceeds threshold
                if new_overall_max < max_kvpr - current_threshold:
                    placement[max_gpu].remove(model)
                    placement[target_gpu].append(model)
                    gpu_sizes[max_gpu] -= s
                    gpu_sizes[target_gpu] += s
                    gpu_pressures[max_gpu] -= p
                    gpu_pressures[target_gpu] += p
                    moved = True
                    break
            
            if moved:
                break
        
        if not moved:
            # Try swapping with models from other GPUs
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu or not placement[target_gpu]:
                    continue
                
                # Try swapping one model from max_gpu with one from target_gpu
                for src_model in placement[max_gpu]:
                    src_idx = models.index(src_model)
                    src_p = model_pressures[src_idx].item()
                    src_s = model_sizes[src_idx].item()
                    
                    for tgt_model in placement[target_gpu]:
                        tgt_idx = models.index(tgt_model)
                        tgt_p = model_pressures[tgt_idx].item()
                        tgt_s = model_sizes[tgt_idx].item()
                        
                        # Check memory constraints
                        if (gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE or
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE):
                            continue
                        
                        # Calculate new KVPRs
                        new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                        new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                        new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                        new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                        
                        if new_max_size <= 0 or new_target_size <= 0:
                            continue
                        
                        new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                        new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                        new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                        
                        # Accept swap if it reduces max KVPR significantly
                        if new_overall_max < max_kvpr - current_threshold:
                            placement[max_gpu].remove(src_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].remove(tgt_model)
                            placement[max_gpu].append(tgt_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].append(src_model)
                            
                            gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = new_max_size
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = new_target_size
                            gpu_pressures[max_gpu] = new_max_pressure
                            gpu_pressures[target_gpu] = new_target_pressure
                            
                            moved = True
                            break
                    
                    if moved:
                        break
                
                if moved:
                    break
        
        if not moved:
            stability_check += 1
            if stability_check >= max_stability:
                break
        else:
            stability_check = 0  # Reset counter on improvement
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != n_models:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {n_models} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import torch
import math
from typing import List, Dict

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    n_models = len(models)
    if n_models == 0:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Extract model properties
    model_sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    model_pressures = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Validate total memory constraint
    total_size = model_sizes.sum().item()
    total_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    if total_size > total_memory:
        raise ValueError(f"Total model size {total_size}GB exceeds total GPU memory {total_memory}GB")
    
    # PARADIGM SHIFT: Continuous Optimization via Normalized Cut & Greedy Refinement
    # Instead of discrete placement heuristics, we treat this as a continuous optimization problem
    # by mapping model attributes to a 2D space (size, pressure) and using a normalized cut approach
    # to partition the space into gpu_num clusters, followed by discrete refinement.
    
    # Step 1: Feature Engineering & Space Transformation
    # Transform (size, pressure) into a normalized space where we can apply clustering
    # Use log-scale to handle variance and normalize by percentiles for robustness
    
    log_sizes = torch.log1p(model_sizes)
    log_pressures = torch.log1p(model_pressures)
    
    # Weighted combination: pressure density (pressure/size) is the key metric, but we also want
    # to cluster models that are "similar" to allow better packing.
    # We create a 2D coordinate: [normalized_pressure_density, normalized_size]
    pressure_density = model_pressures / (model_sizes + 1e-9)
    
    # Normalize features to [0, 1] range for consistent clustering
    pd_norm = (pressure_density - pressure_density.min()) / (pressure_density.max() - pressure_density.min() + 1e-9)
    size_norm = (model_sizes - model_sizes.min()) / (model_sizes.max() - model_sizes.min() + 1e-9)
    
    # Create embedding: high pressure density + small size = high priority for balancing
    # We want to spread high pressure density models across GPUs, but pack similar-sized models
    embedding = torch.stack([pd_norm, size_norm], dim=1)
    
    # Step 2: Iterative Refinement with Memory-Aware Partitioning
    # We use a modified k-means approach that respects memory constraints
    
    # Initialize centroids: spread them across the feature space
    # Use quantiles to ensure good coverage
    centroids = torch.zeros(gpu_num, 2)
    for i in range(gpu_num):
        quantile_idx = (i + 0.5) / gpu_num
        # Pick a point near the corresponding quantile in the embedding
        sorted_pd = torch.argsort(pd_norm)
        idx = sorted_pd[int(quantile_idx * n_models)]
        centroids[i] = embedding[idx]
    
    # Optimization loop: assign models to GPUs, check constraints, adjust centroids
    max_iterations = 100
    best_placement = None
    best_max_kvpr = float('inf')
    
    for iteration in range(max_iterations):
        # Assignment phase: assign each model to the closest centroid that can fit it
        # But we use a dynamic programming approach for the highest priority models
        
        # Sort models by priority: pressure_density * (1 + size_norm) to favor high pressure AND small size
        priority_scores = pressure_density * (1.0 + 0.5 * size_norm)
        sorted_indices = torch.argsort(priority_scores, descending=True)
        
        placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
        gpu_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        gpu_pressures = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        
        # Greedy assignment with backtracking for high-priority models
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            model = models[idx]
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            pressure = model_pressures[idx].item()
            
            # Find valid GPUs
            valid_gpus = [g for g in range(gpu_num) if gpu_sizes[g] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE]
            
            if not valid_gpus:
                # Emergency fallback: find GPU with most free space
                free_spaces = [GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g].item() for g in range(gpu_num)]
                best_gpu = torch.argmax(torch.tensor(free_spaces)).item()
                if gpu_sizes[best_gpu] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    raise ValueError(f"Cannot place model {model.model_name}")
                valid_gpus = [best_gpu]
            
            # Choose GPU that minimizes KVPR increase, with bias toward balanced memory
            best_gpu = None
            best_score = float('inf')
            
            for gpu_id in valid_gpus:
                new_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size
                new_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] + pressure
                
                # KVPR for this GPU
                kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
                
                # Memory utilization penalty: avoid >80% full
                utilization = new_size / GPU_MEM_SIZE
                mem_penalty = 0.0
                if utilization > 0.8:
                    mem_penalty = (utilization - 0.8) * 5.0
                
                # Overall score: KVPR + memory penalty
                score = kvpr + mem_penalty
                
                if score < best_score:
                    best_score = score
                    best_gpu = gpu_id
            
            placement[best_gpu].append(model)
            gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
            gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
        
        # Calculate max KVPR for this placement
        max_kvpr = 0.0
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                kvpr = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
                max_kvpr = max(max_kvpr, kvpr)
        
        # Update best solution
        if max_kvpr < best_max_kvpr:
            best_max_kvpr = max_kvpr
            best_placement = placement
        
        # Update centroids based on current placement
        new_centroids = torch.zeros(gpu_num, 2)
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if placement[g]:
                indices = [models.index(m) for m in placement[g]]
                avg_pd = pressure_density[indices].mean()
                avg_size = size_norm[indices].mean()
                new_centroids[g] = torch.tensor([avg_pd, avg_size])
            else:
                # If empty, re-initialize to a random point in the space
                new_centroids[g] = torch.rand(2)
        
        # Check convergence
        if torch.allclose(centroids, new_centroids, atol=1e-4):
            break
        centroids = new_centroids
    
    # Step 3: Local Search Refinement (Tabu Search with Adaptive Memory)
    # Use the best placement found and apply targeted swaps
    
    final_placement = {i: list(best_placement[i]) for i in range(gpu_num)}
    final_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    final_pressures = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    for g in range(gpu_num):
        for m in final_placement[g]:
            idx = models.index(m)
            final_sizes[g] += model_sizes[idx]
            final_pressures[g] += model_pressures[idx]
    
    # Tabu list to avoid cycling
    tabu_set = set()
    tabu_tenure = 20
    
    max_refinement = 500
    improvement_threshold = 1e-7
    
    for step in range(max_refinement):
        # Calculate current KVPRs
        kvprs = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if final_sizes[g] > 0:
                kvprs[g] = final_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - final_sizes[g])
        
        max_kvpr = torch.max(kvprs).item()
        max_gpu = torch.argmax(kvprs).item()
        
        if not final_placement[max_gpu]:
            break
        
        # Try to improve by moving/swapping from max_gpu
        best_move = None
        best_improvement = 0
        
        # Strategy: Move a model from max_gpu to another GPU
        for model in final_placement[max_gpu]:
            idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressures[idx].item()
            s = model_sizes[idx].item()
            
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if final_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Check tabu
                move_hash = (max_gpu, target_gpu, model.model_name)
                if move_hash in tabu_set:
                    continue
                
                new_max_pressure = final_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_size = final_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_pressure = final_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_size = final_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                if new_max_size <= 0:
                    continue
                
                new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                new_overall = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                
                improvement = max_kvpr - new_overall
                if improvement > best_improvement:
                    best_improvement = improvement
                    best_move = (model, target_gpu, move_hash)
        
        if best_move and best_improvement > improvement_threshold:
            model, target_gpu, move_hash = best_move
            idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressures[idx].item()
            s = model_sizes[idx].item()
            
            final_placement[max_gpu].remove(model)
            final_placement[target_gpu].append(model)
            final_sizes[max_gpu] -= s
            final_sizes[target_gpu] += s
            final_pressures[max_gpu] -= p
            final_pressures[target_gpu] += p
            
            # Add to tabu
            tabu_set.add(move_hash)
            if len(tabu_set) > tabu_tenure:
                tabu_set.pop()
        else:
            # Try swapping
            best_swap = None
            best_swap_improvement = 0
            
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu or not final_placement[target_gpu]:
                    continue
                
                for src_model in final_placement[max_gpu]:
                    src_idx = models.index(src_model)
                    src_p = model_pressures[src_idx].item()
                    src_s = model_sizes[src_idx].item()
                    
                    for tgt_model in final_placement[target_gpu]:
                        tgt_idx = models.index(tgt_model)
                        tgt_p = model_pressures[tgt_idx].item()
                        tgt_s = model_sizes[tgt_idx].item()
                        
                        # Tabu check
                        swap_hash = tuple(sorted([(max_gpu, src_model.model_name), (target_gpu, tgt_model.model_name)]))
                        if swap_hash in tabu_set:
                            continue
                        
                        if (final_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE or
                            final_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE):
                            continue
                        
                        new_max_pressure = final_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                        new_max_size = final_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                        new_target_pressure = final_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                        new_target_size = final_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                        
                        if new_max_size <= 0 or new_target_size <= 0:
                            continue
                        
                        new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                        new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                        new_overall = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                        
                        improvement = max_kvpr - new_overall
                        if improvement > best_swap_improvement:
                            best_swap_improvement = improvement
                            best_swap = (src_model, tgt_model, target_gpu, swap_hash)
            
            if best_swap and best_swap_improvement > improvement_threshold:
                src_model, tgt_model, target_gpu, swap_hash = best_swap
                src_idx = models.index(src_model)
                tgt_idx = models.index(tgt_model)
                src_p = model_pressures[src_idx].item()
                src_s = model_sizes[src_idx].item()
                tgt_p = model_pressures[tgt_idx].item()
                tgt_s = model_sizes[tgt_idx].item()
                
                final_placement[max_gpu].remove(src_model)
                final_placement[target_gpu].remove(tgt_model)
                final_placement[max_gpu].append(tgt_model)
                final_placement[target_gpu].append(src_model)
                
                final_sizes[max_gpu] = final_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                final_sizes[target_gpu] = final_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                final_pressures[max_gpu] = final_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                final_pressures[target_gpu] = final_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                
                tabu_set.add(swap_hash)
                if len(tabu_set) > tabu_tenure:
                    tabu_set.pop()
            else:
                break
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in final_placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    placed_count = sum(len(final_placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != n_models:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {n_models} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return final_placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import torch
import math
from typing import List, Dict

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)


def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    n_models = len(models)
    if n_models == 0:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Extract model properties
    model_sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    model_pressures = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Validate total memory constraint
    total_size = model_sizes.sum().item()
    total_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    if total_size > total_memory:
        raise ValueError(f"Total model size {total_size}GB exceeds total GPU memory {total_memory}GB")
    
    # Calculate theoretical lower bound on max KVPR
    total_pressure = model_pressures.sum().item()
    min_kvpr_lower_bound = total_pressure / (total_memory - total_size)
    
    # PARADIGM SHIFT: Greedy-Balanced Placement with Iterative Refinement (Variant)
    # Modified to use a more aggressive memory utilization heuristic and adjusted thresholds
    
    # Step 1: Calculate a composite metric with stronger emphasis on pressure-to-size ratio
    # Use logarithmic scaling to reduce sensitivity to extreme values
    log_sizes = torch.log1p(model_sizes)
    log_pressures = torch.log1p(model_pressures)
    # Enhanced metric: prioritize models that are high-pressure but small in size
    model_metrics = (log_pressures + 1e-6) / (log_sizes + 1e-6)  # Avoid division by zero
    
    # Normalize and create a priority score with higher sensitivity to pressure
    normalized_pressures = model_pressures / (model_pressures.max() + 1e-6)
    normalized_sizes = model_sizes / (model_sizes.max() + 1e-6)
    priority_scores = normalized_pressures / (normalized_sizes + 0.05)  # Reduced bias toward size
    
    # Step 2: Sort models by priority (descending)
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(priority_scores, descending=True)
    
    # Step 3: Initialize GPU states
    placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    gpu_pressures = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Step 4: Greedy placement with dynamic load balancing
    # Modified scoring: reduce penalty for memory pressure, increase focus on immediate KVPR reduction
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        model = models[idx]
        size = model_sizes[idx].item()
        pressure = model_pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Calculate placement scores for all GPUs
        placement_scores = []
        for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                placement_scores.append(float('inf'))
                continue
            
            current_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id].item()
            current_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id].item()
            
            new_size = current_size + size
            new_pressure = current_pressure + pressure
            
            # Improved KVPR estimation: use a weighted average that penalizes high KVPR more
            if new_size >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                kvpr = float('inf')
            elif current_size == 0:
                kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
            else:
                current_kvpr = current_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - current_size)
                new_kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
                # Enhanced: emphasize reducing high KVPRs via squared difference
                kvpr = new_kvpr + 0.8 * max(0, new_kvpr - current_kvpr) ** 2
            
            # Reduced memory penalty (encourage denser packing)
            free_space_after = GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size
            memory_penalty = 0.5 / (free_space_after + 0.5)  # Lower penalty for less free space
            
            # Final score with stronger focus on KVPR reduction
            score = kvpr + 0.05 * memory_penalty
            placement_scores.append(score)
        
        # Choose GPU with minimum score
        best_gpu = torch.argmin(torch.tensor(placement_scores)).item()
        
        # Place model
        placement[best_gpu].append(model)
        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
        gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Step 5: Iterative refinement with "smart swaps" (Variant: fewer iterations, smarter stopping)
    # Reduce max iterations and tighten convergence threshold
    max_iterations = 1000
    improvement_threshold = 1e-10
    
    for iteration in range(max_iterations):
        # Calculate current KVPRs
        kvprs = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                kvprs[g] = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
        
        if torch.all(kvprs == 0):
            break
        
        max_kvpr = torch.max(kvprs).item()
        max_gpu = torch.argmax(kvprs).item()
        
        if not placement[max_gpu]:
            break
        
        # Find the best improvement: either move one model or swap two models
        
        # Strategy A: Move single model from max_gpu to another GPU
        best_move = None
        best_move_improvement = 0
        
        models_on_max = placement[max_gpu]
        for model in models_on_max:
            idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressures[idx].item()
            s = model_sizes[idx].item()
            
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Calculate improvement
                new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                if new_max_size <= 0:
                    continue
                
                new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                
                improvement = max_kvpr - new_overall_max
                if improvement > best_move_improvement:
                    best_move_improvement = improvement
                    best_move = (model, target_gpu, new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
        
        # Strategy B: Swap two models between max_gpu and another GPU
        best_swap = None
        best_swap_improvement = 0
        
        for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
            if target_gpu == max_gpu or not placement[target_gpu]:
                continue
            
            for src_model in placement[max_gpu]:
                src_idx = models.index(src_model)
                src_p = model_pressures[src_idx].item()
                src_s = model_sizes[src_idx].item()
                
                for tgt_model in placement[target_gpu]:
                    tgt_idx = models.index(tgt_model)
                    tgt_p = model_pressures[tgt_idx].item()
                    tgt_s = model_sizes[tgt_idx].item()
                    
                    # Check validity
                    if (gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE or
                        gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE):
                        continue
                    
                    # Calculate improvement
                    new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                    new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                    new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                    new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                    
                    if new_max_size <= 0 or new_target_size <= 0:
                        continue
                    
                    new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                    new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                    new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                    
                    improvement = max_kvpr - new_overall_max
                    if improvement > best_swap_improvement:
                        best_swap_improvement = improvement
                        best_swap = (src_model, tgt_model, target_gpu, new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
        
        # Choose the best action
        if best_move_improvement > improvement_threshold or best_swap_improvement > improvement_threshold:
            if best_move_improvement >= best_swap_improvement:
                # Perform move
                model, target_gpu, _, _ = best_move
                idx = models.index(model)
                p = model_pressures[idx].item()
                s = model_sizes[idx].item()
                
                placement[max_gpu].remove(model)
                placement[target_gpu].append(model)
                gpu_sizes[max_gpu] -= s
                gpu_sizes[target_gpu] += s
                gpu_pressures[max_gpu] -= p
                gpu_pressures[target_gpu] += p
            else:
                # Perform swap
                src_model, tgt_model, target_gpu, _, _ = best_swap
                src_idx = models.index(src_model)
                tgt_idx = models.index(tgt_model)
                src_p = model_pressures[src_idx].item()
                src_s = model_sizes[src_idx].item()
                tgt_p = model_pressures[tgt_idx].item()
                tgt_s = model_sizes[tgt_idx].item()
                
                placement[max_gpu].remove(src_model)
                placement[target_gpu].remove(tgt_model)
                placement[max_gpu].append(tgt_model)
                placement[target_gpu].append(src_model)
                
                gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                gpu_pressures[max_gpu] = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                gpu_pressures[target_gpu] = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
        else:
            break
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != n_models:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {n_models} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import torch
import math
from typing import List, Dict

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)


def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    n_models = len(models)
    if n_models == 0:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Extract model properties
    model_sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    model_pressures = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Validate total memory constraint
    total_size = model_sizes.sum().item()
    total_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    if total_size > total_memory:
        raise ValueError(f"Total model size {total_size}GB exceeds total GPU memory {total_memory}GB")
    
    # Calculate theoretical lower bound on max KVPR
    total_pressure = model_pressures.sum().item()
    min_kvpr_lower_bound = total_pressure / (total_memory - total_size)
    
    # Strategy: Use a hybrid approach combining:
    # 1. Constraint programming to find optimal KVPR threshold
    # 2. Max-flow based assignment for that threshold
    # 3. Local optimization with refined heuristics
    
    def can_achieve_kvpr(target_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        """
        Check feasibility using a multi-stage packing algorithm with adjusted thresholds:
        - Reduced buffer for small models (7GB instead of 8GB)
        - Smaller threshold for medium models (16GB instead of 14GB)
        - Higher priority for memory utilization in small model placement
        """
        # Adjusted effective weight with bias toward memory preservation
        weights = model_pressures + target_kvpr * model_sizes
        capacity = 80.0 * target_kvpr
        
        # Sort models by size (descending) for better packing
        sorted_indices = torch.argsort(model_sizes, descending=True)
        
        bins = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        bin_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        
        # Separate models by size categories with refined thresholds
        large_models = []
        medium_models = []
        small_models = []
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            if size > 25:  # Slightly stricter for large
                large_models.append(idx)
            elif size > 16:  # Slightly looser for medium
                medium_models.append(idx)
            else:
                small_models.append(idx)
        
        # Pack large models first (most constrained)
        for idx in large_models:
            weight = weights[idx].item()
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            
            valid_bins = []
            for b in range(gpu_num):
                if bin_sizes[b] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE and bins[b] + weight <= capacity:
                    valid_bins.append(b)
            
            if not valid_bins:
                return False
            
            # Best-fit for large models, but prefer bins with higher residual memory
            best_bin = min(valid_bins, key=lambda b: (capacity - bins[b], GPU_MEM_SIZE - bin_sizes[b]))
            bins[best_bin] += weight
            bin_sizes[best_bin] += size
        
        # Pack medium models with lookahead and memory buffer
        for idx in medium_models:
            weight = weights[idx].item()
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            
            valid_bins = []
            for b in range(gpu_num):
                if bin_sizes[b] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE and bins[b] + weight <= capacity:
                    # Enforce buffer for future small models: leave at least 7GB free
                    free_after = GPU_MEM_SIZE - (bin_sizes[b] + size)
                    if free_after >= 7 or len(small_models) == 0:
                        valid_bins.append(b)
            
            if not valid_bins:
                return False
            
            # Choose bin with most free space (to accommodate small models)
            best_bin = max(valid_bins, key=lambda b: GPU_MEM_SIZE - bin_sizes[b])
            bins[best_bin] += weight
            bin_sizes[best_bin] += size
        
        # Pack small models with memory-first priority and higher density focus
        for idx in small_models:
            weight = weights[idx].item()
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            
            valid_bins = []
            for b in range(gpu_num):
                if bin_sizes[b] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE and bins[b] + weight <= capacity:
                    # Favor bins with more free space (memory-first), but also consider pressure density
                    valid_bins.append(b)
            
            if not valid_bins:
                return False
            
            # First-fit, but prefer bins with highest residual memory
            best_bin = min(valid_bins, key=lambda b: GPU_MEM_SIZE - bin_sizes[b])
            bins[best_bin] += weight
            bin_sizes[best_bin] += size
        
        return True
    
    # Binary search for optimal KVPR
    low = min_kvpr_lower_bound
    high = max(total_pressure / 0.1, total_pressure / (gpu_num * 2.5))
    
    # Coarse search
    for _ in range(15):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if can_achieve_kvpr(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    # Fine-tune with more iterations
    for _ in range(120):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if can_achieve_kvpr(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    target_kvpr = high

    # Construct placement using the same packing strategy
    weights = model_pressures + target_kvpr * model_sizes
    
    placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    gpu_pressures = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Sort by size categories
    model_indices = list(range(n_models))
    model_indices.sort(key=lambda i: model_sizes[i].item(), reverse=True)
    
    # Separate by size with adjusted thresholds
    large_indices = [i for i in model_indices if model_sizes[i].item() > 25]
    medium_indices = [i for i in model_indices if 16 < model_sizes[i].item() <= 25]
    small_indices = [i for i in model_indices if model_sizes[i].item() <= 16]
    
    def place_models(indices, placement_strategy="best_fit"):
        for idx in indices:
            model = models[idx]
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            pressure = model_pressures[idx].item()
            
            valid_gpus = []
            for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    valid_gpus.append(gpu_id)
            
            if not valid_gpus:
                # Emergency placement: find GPU with most free space
                free_spaces = [GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g].item() for g in range(gpu_num)]
                best_gpu = torch.argmax(torch.tensor(free_spaces)).item()
                if gpu_sizes[best_gpu] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    raise ValueError(f"Cannot place model {model.model_name}")
                valid_gpus = [best_gpu]
            
            if placement_strategy == "best_fit":
                # Minimize resulting KVPR
                best_gpu = None
                best_kvpr = float('inf')
                for gpu_id in valid_gpus:
                    new_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] + pressure
                    new_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size
                    if new_size >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                        continue
                    new_kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
                    if new_kvpr < best_kvpr:
                        best_kvpr = new_kvpr
                        best_gpu = gpu_id
            elif placement_strategy == "most_space":
                # Maximize remaining space
                best_gpu = max(valid_gpus, key=lambda g: GPU_MEM_SIZE - (gpu_sizes[g] + size))
            else:  # first_fit
                best_gpu = valid_gpus[0]
            
            placement[best_gpu].append(model)
            gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
            gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Place in order: large, medium, small
    place_models(large_indices, "best_fit")
    place_models(medium_indices, "best_fit")
    place_models(small_indices, "most_space")
    
    # Local search: iterative improvement with enhanced swapping
    max_iterations = 2500
    improvement_threshold = 1e-8
    
    for iteration in range(max_iterations):
        # Find GPU with maximum KVPR
        kvprs = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                kvprs[g] = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
        
        if torch.all(kvprs == 0):
            break
        
        max_kvpr = torch.max(kvprs).item()
        max_gpu = torch.argmax(kvprs).item()
        
        if not placement[max_gpu]:
            break
        
        moved = False
        
        # Try moving models from max_gpu
        models_on_max = placement[max_gpu]
        
        # Sort by pressure/size ratio, but give higher weight to models with large impact
        model_impacts = []
        for model in models_on_max:
            idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressures[idx].item()
            s = model_sizes[idx].item()
            ratio = p / (s + 1e-9)
            # Add secondary heuristic: favor models that are both large and high pressure
            impact_score = p * (1.0 + 0.3 * (s / 20.0))  # Increased weight on size
            model_impacts.append((impact_score, ratio, model, p, s))
        
        model_impacts.sort(reverse=True)
        
        for _, _, model, p, s in model_impacts:
            # Try moving to other GPUs
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Calculate new KVPRs
                new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                if new_max_size <= 0:
                    continue
                
                new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                
                # Accept if improvement
                if new_overall_max < max_kvpr - improvement_threshold:
                    placement[max_gpu].remove(model)
                    placement[target_gpu].append(model)
                    gpu_sizes[max_gpu] -= s
                    gpu_sizes[target_gpu] += s
                    gpu_pressures[max_gpu] -= p
                    gpu_pressures[target_gpu] += p
                    moved = True
                    break
            
            if moved:
                break
        
        if not moved:
            # Try swapping between max_gpu and another GPU with better load balance
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu or not placement[target_gpu]:
                    continue
                
                # Try swapping models
                for src_model in placement[max_gpu]:
                    src_idx = models.index(src_model)
                    src_p = model_pressures[src_idx].item()
                    src_s = model_sizes[src_idx].item()
                    
                    for tgt_model in placement[target_gpu]:
                        tgt_idx = models.index(tgt_model)
                        tgt_p = model_pressures[tgt_idx].item()
                        tgt_s = model_sizes[tgt_idx].item()
                        
                        # Check if swap is valid
                        if (gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE or
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE):
                            continue
                        
                        # Calculate new KVPRs
                        new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                        new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                        new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                        new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                        
                        if new_max_size <= 0 or new_target_size <= 0:
                            continue
                        
                        new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                        new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                        new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                        
                        # Accept swap if it reduces the maximum KVPR significantly
                        if new_overall_max < max_kvpr - improvement_threshold:
                            # Perform swap
                            placement[max_gpu].remove(src_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].remove(tgt_model)
                            placement[max_gpu].append(tgt_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].append(src_model)
                            
                            gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                            gpu_pressures[max_gpu] = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                            gpu_pressures[target_gpu] = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                            
                            moved = True
                            break
                    
                    if moved:
                        break
                
                if moved:
                    break
        
        if not moved:
            break
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != n_models:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {n_models} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import torch
import math
from typing import List, Dict

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    n_models = len(models)
    if n_models == 0:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Extract model properties
    model_sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    model_pressures = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Validate total memory constraint
    total_size = model_sizes.sum().item()
    total_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    if total_size > total_memory:
        raise ValueError(f"Total model size {total_size}GB exceeds total GPU memory {total_memory}GB")
    
    # VARIANT: PARADIGM SHIFT with Adjusted Prioritization & Adaptive Refinement
    # Key changes:
    # - Use pressure-to-size ratio with logarithmic scaling for better sensitivity
    # - Introduce adaptive threshold decay in refinement
    # - Add soft memory buffer (5% headroom) to avoid edge cases
    # - Modify swap strategy: prioritize high-pressure models on high-KVPR GPUs
    # - Use geometric progression for refinement iterations
    
    # Step 1: Prioritize models using logarithmic pressure-to-size ratio
    # Enhances sensitivity for small-size, high-pressure models
    log_pressure_to_size = torch.log1p(model_pressures) / (torch.log1p(model_sizes) + 1e-9)
    sorted_indices = torch.argsort(log_pressure_to_size, descending=True)
    
    # Step 2: Initial Assignment with Soft Memory Buffer (5% headroom)
    placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    gpu_pressures = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
    memory_buffer = 0.05 * GPU_MEM_SIZE  # 5% buffer for safety
    
    for idx in sorted_indices:
        model = models[idx]
        size = model_sizes[idx].item()
        pressure = model_pressures[idx].item()
        
        # Find valid GPUs with buffer allowance
        valid_mask = (gpu_sizes + size) <= (GPU_MEM_SIZE - memory_buffer)
        if not valid_mask.any():
            raise ValueError(f"Cannot place model {model.model_name} due to memory overflow (buffered)")
        
        valid_indices = torch.where(valid_mask)[0]
        
        # Calculate resulting KVPR with buffer
        new_sizes = gpu_sizes[valid_indices] + size
        new_pressures = gpu_pressures[valid_indices] + pressure
        kvprs = new_pressures / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_sizes - memory_buffer)
        
        # Best-Fit: minimize KVPR, with tie-breaker favoring free space
        free_spaces = GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_sizes - memory_buffer
        scores = kvprs + (1e-7 / (free_spaces + 1e-9))
        
        best_idx = torch.argmin(scores).item()
        best_gpu = valid_indices[best_idx].item()
        
        placement[best_gpu].append(model)
        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
        gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Step 3: Adaptive Iterative Refinement with Geometric Decay
    # Instead of fixed iterations, use decreasing thresholds and variable depth
    max_iterations = 500
    base_improvement_threshold = 1e-6
    decay_factor = 0.85  # Each iteration reduces threshold by 15%
    
    for iteration in range(max_iterations):
        # Calculate current KVPRs
        kvprs = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                kvprs[g] = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g] - memory_buffer)
        
        if torch.all(kvprs == 0):
            break
        
        max_kvpr = torch.max(kvprs).item()
        max_gpu = torch.argmax(kvprs).item()
        
        if not placement[max_gpu]:
            break
        
        # Adaptive threshold
        current_threshold = base_improvement_threshold * (decay_factor ** iteration)
        
        moved = False
        
        # Strategy A: Move high-impact models from max_gpu
        # Sort by (pressure * size) to target most impactful candidates
        models_on_max = placement[max_gpu]
        model_impacts = []
        for model in models_on_max:
            idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressures[idx].item()
            s = model_sizes[idx].item()
            impact = p * s  # Focus on models that contribute most to KVPR
            model_impacts.append((impact, model, p, s))
        
        model_impacts.sort(reverse=True)
        
        for _, model, p, s in model_impacts:
            # Try to move to any other GPU
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE - memory_buffer:
                    continue
                
                # New KVPRs after move
                new_max_p = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_s = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_p = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_s = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                if new_max_s <= 0:
                    continue
                
                new_max_kvpr = new_max_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_s - memory_buffer)
                new_target_kvpr = new_target_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_s - memory_buffer)
                
                # Check other GPUs' KVPRs
                other_max = 0.0
                for g in range(gpu_num):
                    if g != max_gpu and g != target_gpu and gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                        kvpr = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g] - memory_buffer)
                        if kvpr > other_max:
                            other_max = kvpr
