                new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr, other_max)
                
                if new_overall_max < max_kvpr - current_threshold:
                    # Apply move
                    placement[max_gpu].remove(model)
                    placement[target_gpu].append(model)
                    gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = new_max_s
                    gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = new_target_s
                    gpu_pressures[max_gpu] = new_max_p
                    gpu_pressures[target_gpu] = new_target_p
                    moved = True
                    break
            
            if moved:
                break
        
        # Strategy B: Pairwise swaps with priority on high-pressure models
        if not moved:
            # Only consider swaps if max_gpu has at least 2 models
            if len(placement[max_gpu]) < 2:
                break
            
            # Sort source models by pressure (descending), target models by pressure (ascending)
            src_models = sorted(placement[max_gpu], key=lambda m: m.req_rate / m.slo, reverse=True)
            for src_model in src_models:
                src_idx = models.index(src_model)
                src_p = model_pressures[src_idx].item()
                src_s = model_sizes[src_idx].item()
                
                for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                    if target_gpu == max_gpu or not placement[target_gpu]:
                        continue
                    
                    # Sort target models by pressure (ascending) to swap in low-impact models
                    tgt_models = sorted(placement[target_gpu], key=lambda m: m.req_rate / m.slo)
                    
                    for tgt_model in tgt_models:
                        tgt_idx = models.index(tgt_model)
                        tgt_p = model_pressures[tgt_idx].item()
                        tgt_s = model_sizes[tgt_idx].item()
                        
                        # Check memory after swap
                        if (gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE - memory_buffer or
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE - memory_buffer):
                            continue
                        
                        # Calculate new KVPRs
                        new_max_p = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                        new_max_s = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                        new_target_p = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                        new_target_s = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                        
                        if new_max_s <= 0 or new_target_s <= 0:
                            continue
                        
                        new_max_kvpr = new_max_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_s - memory_buffer)
                        new_target_kvpr = new_target_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_s - memory_buffer)
                        
                        other_max = 0.0
                        for g in range(gpu_num):
                            if g != max_gpu and g != target_gpu and gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                                kvpr = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g] - memory_buffer)
                                if kvpr > other_max:
                                    other_max = kvpr
                        
                        new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr, other_max)
                        
                        if new_overall_max < max_kvpr - current_threshold:
                            # Apply swap
                            placement[max_gpu].remove(src_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].remove(tgt_model)
                            placement[max_gpu].append(tgt_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].append(src_model)
                            
                            gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = new_max_s
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = new_target_s
                            gpu_pressures[max_gpu] = new_max_p
                            gpu_pressures[target_gpu] = new_target_p
                            
                            moved = True
                            break
                    
                    if moved:
                        break
                
                if moved:
                    break
        
        if not moved:
            break  # Converged
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != n_models:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {n_models} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Dict, List
import heapq

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    # Precompute pressure and size for each model
    # Pressure = req_rate / slo
    model_pressure = [m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models]
    model_size = [m.model_size for m in models]
    n_models = len(models)
    
    # Calculate total pressure and total size
    total_pressure = sum(model_pressure)
    total_size = sum(model_size)
    
    # Check if total size exceeds total GPU memory
    if total_size > gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE:
        raise ValueError("Total model size exceeds total GPU memory")
    
    # Use binary search on target KVPR value
    # For a given target KVPR, check if we can place all models such that each GPU's KVPR <= target
    # KVPR <= T => sum(pressure) / (80 - sum(size)) <= T
    # => sum(pressure) <= T * (80 - sum(size))
    # => sum(pressure) + T * sum(size) <= 80 * T
    # => sum(pressure + T * size) <= 80 * T
    
    def can_achieve_kvpr(target_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        """Check if we can place all models with max KVPR <= target_kvpr"""
        
        # Greedy bin packing with modified weights
        # Weight for model i: pressure_i + target_kvpr * size_i
        # Capacity of each GPU: 80 * target_kvpr
        
        weights = [model_pressure[i] + target_kvpr * model_size[i] for i in range(n_models)]
        capacity = 80 * target_kvpr
        
        # Sort models by weight descending (heaviest first)
        indexed_weights = sorted([(w, i) for i, w in enumerate(weights)], reverse=True)
        
        # Greedy first-fit decreasing with memory-based priority
        bins = [0.0] * gpu_num
        bin_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
        
        for weight, idx in indexed_weights:
            size = model_size[idx]
            pressure = model_pressure[idx]
            
            # Find first bin that can fit this model
            placed = False
            # Try GPUs in order of decreasing available memory (best fit heuristic)
            gpu_candidates = sorted(range(gpu_num), key=lambda x: GPU_MEM_SIZE - bin_sizes[x], reverse=True)
            
            for b in gpu_candidates:
                if bin_sizes[b] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                if bins[b] + weight <= capacity + 1e-9:
                    bins[b] += weight
                    bin_sizes[b] += size
                    placed = True
                    break
            
            if not placed:
                return False
        
        return True
    
    # Binary search for optimal KVPR
    low = 0.0
    high = total_pressure / (gpu_num * 0.1)  # Looser upper bound
    
    # Refine upper bound with a more balanced round-robin
    temp_bins = [0.0] * gpu_num
    temp_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
    temp_model_indices = list(range(n_models))
    
    # Shuffle to avoid bias
    import random
    random.seed(42)  # Fixed seed for reproducibility
    random.shuffle(temp_model_indices)
    
    for idx in temp_model_indices:
        # Find GPU with minimum current load (by KVPR, but favor memory availability)
        best_gpu = -1
        best_kvpr = float('inf')
        best_free_mem = -1
        
        for b in range(gpu_num):
            if temp_sizes[b] >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            free_mem = GPU_MEM_SIZE - temp_sizes[b]
            kvpr = temp_bins[b] / free_mem if free_mem > 0 else float('inf')
            # Prefer higher free memory when KVPRs are close
            if kvpr < best_kvpr or (abs(kvpr - best_kvpr) < 1e-9 and free_mem > best_free_mem):
                best_kvpr = kvpr
                best_free_mem = free_mem
                best_gpu = b
        
        if best_gpu == -1:
            return {}  # Impossible
        
        temp_bins[best_gpu] += model_pressure[idx]
        temp_sizes[best_gpu] += model_size[idx]
    
    high = max(
        temp_bins[b] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - temp_sizes[b]) 
        for b in range(gpu_num) 
        if temp_sizes[b] < GPU_MEM_SIZE
    )
    high = max(high, low * 2)  # Ensure high > low
    
    # Binary search with slightly more precision
    for _ in range(65):  # Increased iterations for higher precision
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if can_achieve_kvpr(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    target_kvpr = high
    
    # Now construct the actual placement using the target KVPR
    # Use the same greedy approach but track actual assignments
    weights = [model_pressure[i] + target_kvpr * model_size[i] for i in range(n_models)]
    indexed_weights = sorted([(w, i) for i, w in enumerate(weights)], reverse=True)
    
    placement = {gpu_id: [] for gpu_id in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
    gpu_pressures = [0.0] * gpu_num
    
    for weight, idx in indexed_weights:
        model = models[idx]
        size = model_size[idx]
        pressure = model_pressure[idx]
        
        # Find best GPU that minimizes resulting KVPR while respecting constraints
        best_gpu = None
        best_kvpr = float('inf')
        
        # Try GPUs in order of decreasing free memory (to reduce fragmentation)
        gpu_candidates = sorted(range(gpu_num), key=lambda x: GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[x], reverse=True)
        
        for gpu_id in gpu_candidates:
            if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            
            new_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] + pressure
            new_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size
            new_kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
            
            # Prefer GPUs with lower current KVPR and higher free memory
            if new_kvpr < best_kvpr or (abs(new_kvpr - best_kvpr) < 1e-9 and gpu_sizes[gpu_id] < gpu_sizes[best_gpu] if best_gpu is not None else True):
                best_kvpr = new_kvpr
                best_gpu = gpu_id
        
        if best_gpu is None:
            # Fallback: find any GPU that fits
            for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    best_gpu = gpu_id
                    break
        
        if best_gpu is None:
            raise ValueError(f"Cannot place model {model.model_name}")
        
        placement[best_gpu].append(model)
        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
        gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    # Ensure all models are placed
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != len(models):
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {len(models)} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import math
from typing import List, Dict
import heapq

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)


def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    # Precompute pressure and size for each model
    model_pressure = [m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models]
    model_size = [m.model_size for m in models]
    n_models = len(models)
    
    # Calculate theoretical lower bound for optimal KVPR
    total_pressure = sum(model_pressure)
    total_size = sum(model_size)
    total_free_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_size
    
    # If total memory is zero or negative, impossible
    if total_free_memory <= 0:
        raise ValueError("Total model size exceeds total GPU memory")
    
    # Lower bound on max KVPR
    min_kvpr_lower_bound = total_pressure / total_free_memory
    
    # Use binary search on target KVPR value
    # For a given target KVPR, check if we can place all models such that each GPU's KVPR <= target
    # KVPR <= T => sum(pressure) / (80 - sum(size)) <= T
    # => sum(pressure) <= T * (80 - sum(size))
    # => sum(pressure) + T * sum(size) <= 80 * T
    # => sum(pressure + T * size) <= 80 * T
    
    def can_achieve_kvpr(target_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        """Check if we can place all models with max KVPR <= target_kvpr"""
        
        # Greedy bin packing with modified weights
        # Weight for model i: pressure_i + target_kvpr * size_i
        # Capacity of each GPU: 80 * target_kvpr
        
        weights = [model_pressure[i] + target_kvpr * model_size[i] for i in range(n_models)]
        capacity = 80 * target_kvpr
        
        # Sort models by weight descending (heaviest first)
        # Also track original indices
        indexed_weights = sorted([(w, i) for i, w in enumerate(weights)], reverse=True)
        
        # Greedy first-fit decreasing
        bins = [0.0] * gpu_num
        bin_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
        bin_models = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
        
        for weight, idx in indexed_weights:
            size = model_size[idx]
            pressure = model_pressure[idx]
            
            # Find first bin that can fit this model
            placed = False
            # Try to place in GPU with most available memory first (better fit)
            gpu_candidates = sorted(range(gpu_num), key=lambda x: GPU_MEM_SIZE - bin_sizes[x], reverse=True)
            
            for b in gpu_candidates:
                if bin_sizes[b] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                if bins[b] + weight <= capacity + 1e-10:  # Slightly relaxed tolerance
                    bins[b] += weight
                    bin_sizes[b] += size
                    bin_models[b].append(idx)
                    placed = True
                    break
            
            if not placed:
                return False
        
        # Verify all models placed
        if sum(len(b) for b in bin_models) != n_models:
            return False
            
        return True
    
    # Binary search for optimal KVPR
    low = min_kvpr_lower_bound
    high = total_pressure / (gpu_num * 0.1)  # Looser upper bound
    
    # Refine upper bound with a more balanced round-robin
    temp_bins = [0.0] * gpu_num
    temp_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
    temp_model_indices = list(range(n_models))
    # Shuffle to avoid bias
    import random
    random.seed(42)  # Fixed seed for reproducibility
    random.shuffle(temp_model_indices)
    
    for idx in temp_model_indices:
        # Find GPU with minimum current load (by KVPR, but favor memory availability)
        best_gpu = -1
        best_kvpr = float('inf')
        best_free_mem = -1
        
        for b in range(gpu_num):
            if temp_sizes[b] >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            free_mem = GPU_MEM_SIZE - temp_sizes[b]
            kvpr = temp_bins[b] / free_mem if free_mem > 0 else float('inf')
            # Prefer higher free memory when KVPRs are close
            if kvpr < best_kvpr or (abs(kvpr - best_kvpr) < 1e-9 and free_mem > best_free_mem):
                best_kvpr = kvpr
                best_free_mem = free_mem
                best_gpu = b
        
        if best_gpu == -1:
            return False  # Impossible
        
        temp_bins[best_gpu] += model_pressure[idx]
        temp_sizes[best_gpu] += model_size[idx]
    
    high = max(
        temp_bins[b] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - temp_sizes[b]) 
        for b in range(gpu_num) 
        if temp_sizes[b] < GPU_MEM_SIZE
    )
    high = max(high, low * 2.5)  # Looser upper bound
    
    # Binary search with fewer iterations due to tighter convergence
    for _ in range(50):  # Slightly fewer iterations
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if can_achieve_kvpr(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    target_kvpr = high
    
    # Now construct the actual placement using the target KVPR
    # Use the same greedy approach but track actual assignments
    weights = [model_pressure[i] + target_kvpr * model_size[i] for i in range(n_models)]
    indexed_weights = sorted([(w, i) for i, w in enumerate(weights)], reverse=True)
    
    placement = {gpu_id: [] for gpu_id in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
    gpu_pressures = [0.0] * gpu_num
    
    for weight, idx in indexed_weights:
        model = models[idx]
        size = model_size[idx]
        pressure = model_pressure[idx]
        
        # Find best GPU that minimizes resulting KVPR while respecting constraints
        best_gpu = None
        best_kvpr = float('inf')
        
        # Try GPUs in order of decreasing free memory (to reduce fragmentation)
        gpu_candidates = sorted(range(gpu_num), key=lambda x: GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[x], reverse=True)
        
        for gpu_id in gpu_candidates:
            if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            
            new_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] + pressure
            new_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size
            new_kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
            
            # Prefer GPUs that are already balanced in load
            # Slight preference for GPUs with lower current pressure
            if new_kvpr < best_kvpr or (abs(new_kvpr - best_kvpr) < 1e-9 and gpu_sizes[gpu_id] < gpu_sizes[best_gpu] if best_gpu is not None else True):
                best_kvpr = new_kvpr
                best_gpu = gpu_id
        
        if best_gpu is None:
            # Fallback: find any GPU that fits
            for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    best_gpu = gpu_id
                    break
        
        if best_gpu is None:
            raise ValueError(f"Cannot place model {model.model_name}")
        
        placement[best_gpu].append(model)
        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
        gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Final improvement: local search to reduce max KVPR
    improved = True
    max_iterations = 1000  # Reduced iterations for speed
    iterations = 0
    
    while improved and iterations < max_iterations:
        improved = False
        iterations += 1
        
        # Find GPU with highest KVPR
        max_kvpr = -1
        max_gpu = -1
        for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
            if not placement[gpu_id]:
                continue
            size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id]
            pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id]
            if size >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            kvpr = pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - size)
            if kvpr > max_kvpr:
                max_kvpr = kvpr
                max_gpu = gpu_id
        
        if max_gpu == -1:
            break
        
        # Try to move a model from max_gpu to another GPU
        # Prioritize models with high pressure-to-size ratio (more impact)
        model_candidates = []
        for i, model in enumerate(placement[max_gpu]):
            p = model_pressure[models.index(model)]
            s = model_size[models.index(model)]
            ratio = p / (s + 1e-9)  # Avoid division by zero
            model_candidates.append((ratio, i, model))
        
        # Sort by pressure-to-size ratio descending
        model_candidates.sort(reverse=True)
        
        for _, i, model in model_candidates:
            model_idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressure[model_idx]
            s = model_size[model_idx]
            
            # Try to place in any other GPU
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Calculate new KVPRs
                new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                if new_max_size <= 0 or new_target_size >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Avoid moving models that would create imbalance
                old_max = max_kvpr
                old_target_kvpr = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[target_gpu]) if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] < GPU_MEM_SIZE else 0
                
                new_max = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size) if new_max_size < GPU_MEM_SIZE else float('inf')
                new_target = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                
                new_max_kvpr = max(new_max, new_target)
                
                # Only accept if it reduces the maximum KVPR significantly
                if new_max_kvpr < old_max - 1e-9:
                    # Perform swap
                    placement[max_gpu].pop(i)
                    placement[target_gpu].append(model)
                    gpu_sizes[max_gpu] -= s
                    gpu_sizes[target_gpu] += s
                    gpu_pressures[max_gpu] -= p
                    gpu_pressures[target_gpu] += p
                    improved = True
                    break
            
            if improved:
                break
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    # Ensure all models are placed
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != len(models):
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {len(models)} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import math
from typing import List, Dict
import heapq

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)


def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    # Precompute pressure and size for each model
    model_pressure = [m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models]
    model_size = [m.model_size for m in models]
    n_models = len(models)
    
    # Calculate theoretical lower bound for optimal KVPR
    total_pressure = sum(model_pressure)
    total_size = sum(model_size)
    total_free_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_size
    
    # If total memory is zero or negative, impossible
    if total_free_memory <= 0:
        raise ValueError("Total model size exceeds total GPU memory")
    
    # Lower bound on max KVPR
    min_kvpr_lower_bound = total_pressure / total_free_memory
    
    # Use binary search on target KVPR value
    # For a given target KVPR, check if we can place all models such that each GPU's KVPR <= target
    # KVPR <= T => sum(pressure) / (80 - sum(size)) <= T
    # => sum(pressure) <= T * (80 - sum(size))
    # => sum(pressure) + T * sum(size) <= 80 * T
    # => sum(pressure + T * size) <= 80 * T
    
    def can_achieve_kvpr(target_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        """Check if we can place all models with max KVPR <= target_kvpr"""
        
        # Greedy bin packing with modified weights
        # Weight for model i: pressure_i + target_kvpr * size_i
        # Capacity of each GPU: 80 * target_kvpr
        
        weights = [model_pressure[i] + target_kvpr * model_size[i] for i in range(n_models)]
        capacity = 80 * target_kvpr
        
        # Sort models by weight descending (heaviest first)
        # Also track original indices
        indexed_weights = sorted([(w, i) for i, w in enumerate(weights)], reverse=True)
        
        # Greedy first-fit decreasing
        bins = [0.0] * gpu_num
        bin_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
        bin_models = [[] for _ in range(gpu_num)]
        
        for weight, idx in indexed_weights:
            size = model_size[idx]
            pressure = model_pressure[idx]
            
            # Find first bin that can fit this model
            placed = False
            # Try to place in GPU with most available memory first (better fit)
            gpu_candidates = sorted(range(gpu_num), key=lambda x: GPU_MEM_SIZE - bin_sizes[x], reverse=True)
            
            for b in gpu_candidates:
                if bin_sizes[b] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                if bins[b] + weight <= capacity + 1e-9:
                    bins[b] += weight
                    bin_sizes[b] += size
                    bin_models[b].append(idx)
                    placed = True
                    break
            
            if not placed:
                return False
        
        # Verify all models placed
        if sum(len(b) for b in bin_models) != n_models:
            return False
            
        return True
    
    # Binary search for optimal KVPR
    low = min_kvpr_lower_bound
    high = total_pressure / (gpu_num * 0.1)  # Very loose upper bound
    
    # Refine upper bound with a more balanced round-robin
    temp_bins = [0.0] * gpu_num
    temp_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
    temp_model_indices = list(range(n_models))
    # Shuffle to avoid bias
    import random
    random.shuffle(temp_model_indices)
    
    for idx in temp_model_indices:
        # Find GPU with minimum current load (by KVPR, but favor memory availability)
        best_gpu = -1
        best_kvpr = float('inf')
        best_free_mem = -1
        
        for b in range(gpu_num):
            if temp_sizes[b] >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            free_mem = GPU_MEM_SIZE - temp_sizes[b]
            kvpr = temp_bins[b] / free_mem if free_mem > 0 else float('inf')
            # Prefer higher free memory when KVPRs are close
            if kvpr < best_kvpr or (abs(kvpr - best_kvpr) < 1e-9 and free_mem > best_free_mem):
                best_kvpr = kvpr
                best_free_mem = free_mem
                best_gpu = b
        
        if best_gpu == -1:
            return False  # Impossible
        
        temp_bins[best_gpu] += model_pressure[idx]
        temp_sizes[best_gpu] += model_size[idx]
    
    high = max(
        temp_bins[b] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - temp_sizes[b]) 
        for b in range(gpu_num) 
        if temp_sizes[b] < GPU_MEM_SIZE
    )
    high = max(high, low * 2)  # Ensure high > low
    
    # Binary search
    for _ in range(60):  # Slightly more iterations for precision
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if can_achieve_kvpr(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    target_kvpr = high
    
    # Now construct the actual placement using the target KVPR
    # Use the same greedy approach but track actual assignments
    weights = [model_pressure[i] + target_kvpr * model_size[i] for i in range(n_models)]
    indexed_weights = sorted([(w, i) for i, w in enumerate(weights)], reverse=True)
    
    placement = {gpu_id: [] for gpu_id in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
    gpu_pressures = [0.0] * gpu_num
    
    for weight, idx in indexed_weights:
        model = models[idx]
        size = model_size[idx]
        pressure = model_pressure[idx]
        
        # Find best GPU that minimizes resulting KVPR while respecting constraints
        best_gpu = None
        best_kvpr = float('inf')
        
        # Try GPUs in order of decreasing free memory (to reduce fragmentation)
        gpu_candidates = sorted(range(gpu_num), key=lambda x: GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[x], reverse=True)
        
        for gpu_id in gpu_candidates:
            if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            
            new_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] + pressure
            new_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size
            new_kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
            
            # Prefer GPUs that are already balanced in load
            if new_kvpr < best_kvpr or (abs(new_kvpr - best_kvpr) < 1e-9 and gpu_sizes[gpu_id] < gpu_sizes[best_gpu] if best_gpu is not None else True):
                best_kvpr = new_kvpr
                best_gpu = gpu_id
        
        if best_gpu is None:
            # Fallback: find any GPU that fits
            for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    best_gpu = gpu_id
                    break
        
        if best_gpu is None:
            raise ValueError(f"Cannot place model {model.model_name}")
        
        placement[best_gpu].append(model)
        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
        gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Final improvement: local search to reduce max KVPR
    improved = True
    max_iterations = 1500  # Slightly more iterations
    iterations = 0
    
    while improved and iterations < max_iterations:
        improved = False
        iterations += 1
        
        # Find GPU with highest KVPR
        max_kvpr = -1
        max_gpu = -1
        for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
            if not placement[gpu_id]:
                continue
            size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id]
            pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id]
            if size >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            kvpr = pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - size)
            if kvpr > max_kvpr:
                max_kvpr = kvpr
                max_gpu = gpu_id
        
        if max_gpu == -1:
            break
        
        # Try to move a model from max_gpu to another GPU
        # Prioritize models with high pressure-to-size ratio (more impact)
        model_candidates = []
        for i, model in enumerate(placement[max_gpu]):
            p = model_pressure[models.index(model)]
            s = model_size[models.index(model)]
            ratio = p / (s + 1e-9)  # Avoid division by zero
            model_candidates.append((ratio, i, model))
        
        # Sort by pressure-to-size ratio descending
        model_candidates.sort(reverse=True)
        
        for _, i, model in model_candidates:
            model_idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressure[model_idx]
            s = model_size[model_idx]
            
            # Try to place in any other GPU
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Calculate new KVPRs
                new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                if new_max_size <= 0 or new_target_size >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Avoid moving models that would create imbalance
                old_max = max_kvpr
                old_target_kvpr = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[target_gpu]) if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] < GPU_MEM_SIZE else 0
                
                new_max = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size) if new_max_size < GPU_MEM_SIZE else float('inf')
                new_target = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                
                new_max_kvpr = max(new_max, new_target)
                
                # Only accept if it reduces the maximum KVPR significantly
                if new_max_kvpr < old_max - 1e-8:
                    # Perform swap
                    placement[max_gpu].pop(i)
                    placement[target_gpu].append(model)
                    gpu_sizes[max_gpu] -= s
                    gpu_sizes[target_gpu] += s
                    gpu_pressures[max_gpu] -= p
                    gpu_pressures[target_gpu] += p
                    improved = True
                    break
            
            if improved:
                break
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    # Ensure all models are placed
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != len(models):
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {len(models)} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    # Precompute pressure and size for each model
    model_pressure = [m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models]
    model_size = [m.model_size for m in models]
    n_models = len(models)
    
    # Calculate total pressure and total size
    total_pressure = sum(model_pressure)
    total_size = sum(model_size)
    
    # Check if total size exceeds total GPU memory
    if total_size > gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE:
        raise ValueError("Total model size exceeds total GPU memory")
    
    # Use binary search on target KVPR value
    # For a given target KVPR, check if we can place all models such that each GPU's KVPR <= target
    # KVPR <= T => sum(pressure) / (80 - sum(size)) <= T
    # => sum(pressure) <= T * (80 - sum(size))
    # => sum(pressure) + T * sum(size) <= 80 * T
    # => sum(pressure + T * size) <= 80 * T
    
    def can_achieve_kvpr(target_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        """Check if we can place all models with max KVPR <= target_kvpr"""
        
        # Greedy bin packing with modified weights
        # Weight for model i: pressure_i + target_kvpr * size_i
        # Capacity of each GPU: 80 * target_kvpr
        
        weights = [model_pressure[i] + target_kvpr * model_size[i] for i in range(n_models)]
        capacity = 80 * target_kvpr
        
        # Sort models by weight descending (heaviest first)
        indexed_weights = sorted([(w, i) for i, w in enumerate(weights)], reverse=True)
        
        # Greedy first-fit decreasing with memory-based priority
        bins = [0.0] * gpu_num
        bin_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
        
        for weight, idx in indexed_weights:
            size = model_size[idx]
            pressure = model_pressure[idx]
            
            # Find first bin that can fit this model
            placed = False
            # Try GPUs in order of decreasing available memory (best fit heuristic)
            gpu_candidates = sorted(range(gpu_num), key=lambda x: GPU_MEM_SIZE - bin_sizes[x], reverse=True)
            
            for b in gpu_candidates:
                if bin_sizes[b] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                if bins[b] + weight <= capacity + 1e-9:
                    bins[b] += weight
                    bin_sizes[b] += size
                    placed = True
                    break
            
            if not placed:
                return False
        
        return True
    
    # Binary search for optimal KVPR
    low = 0.0
    high = total_pressure / (gpu_num * 0.1)  # Looser upper bound
    
    # Refine upper bound with a more balanced round-robin
    temp_bins = [0.0] * gpu_num
    temp_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
    temp_model_indices = list(range(n_models))
    
    # Shuffle to avoid bias
    random.seed(42)  # Fixed seed for reproducibility
    random.shuffle(temp_model_indices)
    
    for idx in temp_model_indices:
        # Find GPU with minimum current load (by KVPR, but favor memory availability)
        best_gpu = -1
        best_kvpr = float('inf')
        best_free_mem = -1
        
        for b in range(gpu_num):
            if temp_sizes[b] >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            free_mem = GPU_MEM_SIZE - temp_sizes[b]
            kvpr = temp_bins[b] / free_mem if free_mem > 0 else float('inf')
            # Prefer higher free memory when KVPRs are close
            if kvpr < best_kvpr or (abs(kvpr - best_kvpr) < 1e-9 and free_mem > best_free_mem):
                best_kvpr = kvpr
                best_free_mem = free_mem
                best_gpu = b
        
        if best_gpu == -1:
            return {}  # Impossible
        
        temp_bins[best_gpu] += model_pressure[idx]
        temp_sizes[best_gpu] += model_size[idx]
    
    high = max(
        temp_bins[b] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - temp_sizes[b]) 
        for b in range(gpu_num) 
        if temp_sizes[b] < GPU_MEM_SIZE
    )
    high = max(high, low * 2)  # Ensure high > low
    
    # Binary search with slightly more precision
    for _ in range(70):  # Increased iterations for higher precision
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if can_achieve_kvpr(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    target_kvpr = high
    
    # Now construct the actual placement using the target KVPR
    # Use the same greedy approach but track actual assignments
    weights = [model_pressure[i] + target_kvpr * model_size[i] for i in range(n_models)]
    indexed_weights = sorted([(w, i) for i, w in enumerate(weights)], reverse=True)
    
    placement = {gpu_id: [] for gpu_id in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
    gpu_pressures = [0.0] * gpu_num
    
    for weight, idx in indexed_weights:
        model = models[idx]
        size = model_size[idx]
        pressure = model_pressure[idx]
        
        # Find best GPU that minimizes resulting KVPR while respecting constraints
        best_gpu = None
        best_kvpr = float('inf')
        
        # Try GPUs in order of decreasing free memory (to reduce fragmentation)
        gpu_candidates = sorted(range(gpu_num), key=lambda x: GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[x], reverse=True)
        
        for gpu_id in gpu_candidates:
            if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            
            new_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] + pressure
            new_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size
            new_kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
            
            # Prefer GPUs with lower current KVPR and higher free memory
            if new_kvpr < best_kvpr or (abs(new_kvpr - best_kvpr) < 1e-9 and (best_gpu is None or gpu_sizes[gpu_id] < gpu_sizes[best_gpu])):
                best_kvpr = new_kvpr
                best_gpu = gpu_id
        
        if best_gpu is None:
            # Fallback: find any GPU that fits
            for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    best_gpu = gpu_id
                    break
        
        if best_gpu is None:
            raise ValueError(f"Cannot place model {model.model_name}")
        
        placement[best_gpu].append(model)
        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
        gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    # Ensure all models are placed
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != len(models):
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {len(models)} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import torch
import math
from typing import List, Dict

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    n_models = len(models)
    if n_models == 0:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Extract model properties
    model_sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    model_pressures = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Validate total memory constraint
    total_size = model_sizes.sum().item()
    total_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    if total_size > total_memory:
        raise ValueError(f"Total model size {total_size}GB exceeds total GPU memory {total_memory}GB")
    
    # PARADIGM SHIFT: Linear Programming with Greedy Rounding and Local Search (Variant)
    # Modifications:
    # - Tighter binary search bounds
    # - Different weight function in feasibility check
    # - Enhanced local search with dynamic priority and early termination
    # - Slight bias toward models with higher pressure-to-size ratio in initial assignment
    # - Smaller improvement threshold and more aggressive swap attempts
    
    # Step 1: Binary search for optimal continuous KVPR (tighter bounds)
    total_pressure = model_pressures.sum().item()
    min_kvpr_lower_bound = total_pressure / (total_memory - total_size)
    
    # Use tighter upper bound based on average + 20% margin
    avg_kvpr = min_kvpr_lower_bound
    max_single_kvpr = max(model_pressures[i].item() / (80.0 - model_sizes[i].item()) for i in range(n_models))
    high = max(avg_kvpr * 1.5, max_single_kvpr * 1.1)
    low = min_kvpr_lower_bound * 0.97
    
    # Pre-calculate model properties
    model_props = [(model_sizes[i].item(), model_pressures[i].item()) for i in range(n_models)]
    
    def is_feasible(target_kvpr: float) -> bool:
        """
        Modified feasibility check: use a more aggressive bin packing heuristic
        with adjusted capacity and tighter KVPR checks.
        """
        capacity = 80.0 * target_kvpr
        epsilon = 1e-8
        
        # Use weight: p_j + target_kvpr * s_j * 1.2 to prioritize memory-heavy models
        modified_weights = [p + target_kvpr * s * 1.2 for s, p in model_props]
        indices = sorted(range(n_models), key=lambda i: modified_weights[i], reverse=True)
        
        bins = []  # [current_weight, current_size, current_pressure]
        
        for idx in indices:
            s, p = model_props[idx]
            mw = modified_weights[idx]
            
            placed = False
            for i in range(len(bins)):
                bin_mw, bin_s, bin_p = bins[i]
                if bin_mw + mw <= capacity + epsilon:
                    new_p = bin_p + p
                    new_s = bin_s + s
                    if new_s < 80.0:
                        new_kvpr = new_p / (80.0 - new_s)
                        if new_kvpr <= target_kvpr + 1e-7:
                            bins[i] = (bin_mw + mw, new_s, new_p)
                            placed = True
                            break
            
            if not placed:
                if mw <= capacity + epsilon:
                    bins.append((mw, s, p))
                else:
                    return False
        
        return len(bins) <= gpu_num
    
    # Binary search with fewer iterations but tighter convergence
    for _ in range(40):
        mid = (low + high) / 2
        if is_feasible(mid):
            high = mid
        else:
            low = mid
    
    target_kvpr = high
    
    # Step 2: Greedy rounding with enhanced sorting
    # Prioritize models with high pressure/size ratio to avoid imbalance
    modified_weights = [p + target_kvpr * s * 1.2 for s, p in model_props]
    indices = sorted(
        range(n_models),
        key=lambda i: (
            modified_weights[i],
            model_pressures[i].item() / (model_sizes[i].item() + 1e-8),
            -model_sizes[i].item()  # favor smaller models for flexibility
        ),
        reverse=True
    )
    
    placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = [0.0] * gpu_num
    gpu_pressures = [0.0] * gpu_num
    
    for idx in indices:
        s, p = model_props[idx]
        model = models[idx]
        
        best_gpu = -1
        best_score = float('inf')
        
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[g] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            
            new_size = gpu_sizes[g] + s
            new_pressure = gpu_pressures[g] + p
            kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
            
            # Heuristic: balance KVPR and memory utilization, with stronger penalty on high KVPR
            memory_utilization = new_size / GPU_MEM_SIZE
            score = kvpr * 1.2 + 0.1 * memory_utilization
            
            if score < best_score:
                best_score = score
                best_gpu = g
        
        if best_gpu == -1:
            # Fallback: pick GPU with most free space
            free_spaces = [GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g] for g in range(gpu_num)]
            best_gpu = torch.argmax(torch.tensor(free_spaces)).item()
        
        placement[best_gpu].append(model)
        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += s
        gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += p
    
    # Step 3: Local Search Refinement (enhanced)
    # More aggressive and adaptive search with dynamic iteration limits
    max_iterations = 800
    improvement_threshold = 1e-7
    patience = 20  # stop if no improvement in last 'patience' iterations
    no_improve_count = 0
    
    for iteration in range(max_iterations):
        # Calculate current KVPRs
        kvprs = []
        for g in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                kvprs.append(gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g]))
            else:
                kvprs.append(0.0)
        
        current_max_kvpr = max(kvprs)
        if current_max_kvpr < 1e-6:
            break
        
        max_gpu = kvprs.index(current_max_kvpr)
        
        if not placement[max_gpu] or len(placement[max_gpu]) == 1:
            break
        
        moved = False
        
        # Try moving models from max_gpu
        models_on_max = placement[max_gpu]
        model_impacts = []
        for m in models_on_max:
            idx = models.index(m)
            p = model_pressures[idx].item()
            s = model_sizes[idx].item()
            # Use impact: pressure * size * (1 + 0.5 * (1 - s/30)) to prefer medium-sized models
            size_factor = 1.0 + 0.5 * (1.0 - min(s, 30) / 30.0)
            impact = p * s * size_factor
            model_impacts.append((impact, m, p, s))
        
        model_impacts.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
        
        for _, model, p, s in model_impacts:
            best_target = -1
            best_improvement = 0.0
            
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Avoid emptying max_gpu unless it's the only model
                if gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s <= 0 and len(placement[max_gpu]) == 1:
                    continue
                
                new_max_p = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_s = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_p = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_s = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                new_max_kvpr = new_max_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_s) if new_max_s > 0 else 0.0
                new_target_kvpr = new_target_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_s)
                
                other_max = 0.0
                for g in range(gpu_num):
                    if g != max_gpu and g != target_gpu and gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                        kvpr = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
                        if kvpr > other_max:
                            other_max = kvpr
                
                potential_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr, other_max)
                improvement = current_max_kvpr - potential_max
                
                if improvement > best_improvement:
                    best_improvement = improvement
                    best_target = target_gpu
            
            if best_target != -1 and best_improvement > improvement_threshold:
                placement[max_gpu].remove(model)
                placement[best_target].append(model)
                
                gpu_sizes[max_gpu] -= s
                gpu_pressures[max_gpu] -= p
                gpu_sizes[best_target] += s
                gpu_pressures[best_target] += p
                moved = True
                no_improve_count = 0
                break
        
        if not moved:
            # Try swaps with higher frequency and better criteria
            for src_model in placement[max_gpu]:
                src_idx = models.index(src_model)
                src_p = model_pressures[src_idx].item()
                src_s = model_sizes[src_idx].item()
                
                for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                    if target_gpu == max_gpu or not placement[target_gpu]:
                        continue
                    
                    for tgt_model in placement[target_gpu]:
                        tgt_idx = models.index(tgt_model)
                        tgt_p = model_pressures[tgt_idx].item()
                        tgt_s = model_sizes[tgt_idx].item()
                        
                        if (gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE or
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s > GPU_MEM_SIZE):
                            continue
                        
                        new_max_p = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - src_p + tgt_p
                        new_max_s = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - src_s + tgt_s
                        new_target_p = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] - tgt_p + src_p
                        new_target_s = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] - tgt_s + src_s
                        
                        new_max_kvpr = new_max_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_s) if new_max_s > 0 else 0.0
                        new_target_kvpr = new_target_p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_s)
                        
                        other_max = 0.0
                        for g in range(gpu_num):
                            if g != max_gpu and g != target_gpu and gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                                kvpr = gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g])
                                if kvpr > other_max:
                                    other_max = kvpr
                        
                        potential_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr, other_max)
                        improvement = current_max_kvpr - potential_max
                        
                        if improvement > improvement_threshold:
                            placement[max_gpu].remove(src_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].remove(tgt_model)
                            placement[max_gpu].append(tgt_model)
                            placement[target_gpu].append(src_model)
                            
                            gpu_sizes[max_gpu] = new_max_s
                            gpu_sizes[target_gpu] = new_target_s
                            gpu_pressures[max_gpu] = new_max_p
                            gpu_pressures[target_gpu] = new_target_p
                            
                            moved = True
                            no_improve_count = 0
                            break
                    
                    if moved:
                        break
                
                if moved:
                    break
        
        if not moved:
            no_improve_count += 1
            if no_improve_count >= patience:
                break
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    placed_count = sum(len(placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != n_models:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {n_models} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import torch
import math
from typing import List, Dict

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)

def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    n_models = len(models)
    if n_models == 0:
        return {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
    
    # Extract model properties
    model_sizes = torch.tensor([m.model_size for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    model_pressures = torch.tensor([m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models], dtype=torch.float32)
    
    # Validate total memory constraint
    total_size = model_sizes.sum().item()
    total_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE
    if total_size > total_memory:
        raise ValueError(f"Total model size {total_size}GB exceeds total GPU memory {total_memory}GB")
    
    # Calculate theoretical lower bound on max KVPR
    total_pressure = model_pressures.sum().item()
    min_kvpr_lower_bound = total_pressure / (total_memory - total_size)
    
    # PARADIGM SHIFT: Simulated Annealing with Enhanced Greedy Initialization and Dynamic Cooling
    # Variant: Emphasis on memory-aware pressure balancing and adaptive neighbor generation

    # Step 1: Generate a smart initial solution using a modified greedy heuristic
    def generate_initial_placement():
        placement = {i: [] for i in range(gpu_num)}
        gpu_sizes = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        gpu_pressures = torch.zeros(gpu_num, dtype=torch.float32)
        
        # Sort models by a composite metric: (pressure / size) * (1 + log(1 + size))
        # This prioritizes models with high pressure per size and penalizes very large models slightly
        size_log = torch.log1p(model_sizes)
        composite_ratios = (model_pressures / (model_sizes + 1e-9)) * (1.0 + size_log)
        sorted_indices = torch.argsort(composite_ratios, descending=True)
        
        for idx in sorted_indices:
            model = models[idx]
            size = model_sizes[idx].item()
            pressure = model_pressures[idx].item()
            
            # Calculate scores for all GPUs
            scores = []
            for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    scores.append(float('inf'))
                    continue
                
                new_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size
                new_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] + pressure
                kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
                scores.append(kvpr)
            
            # Use a hybrid selection: prefer low KVPR, but allow some randomness early on
            scores_tensor = torch.tensor(scores)
            best_gpu = torch.argmin(scores_tensor).item()
            
            placement[best_gpu].append(model)
            gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
            gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
        
        return placement
    
    # Step 2: Calculate current KVPR for a placement
    def calculate_max_kvpr(placement):
        max_kvpr = 0.0
        for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
            gpu_models = placement[gpu_id]
            if not gpu_models:
                continue
            size = sum(m.model_size for m in gpu_models)
            pressure = sum(m.req_rate / m.slo for m in gpu_models)
            kvpr = pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - size)
            max_kvpr = max(max_kvpr, kvpr)
        return max_kvpr
    
    # Step 3: Generate a neighbor solution by moving or swapping models
    def generate_neighbor(placement, temperature):
        new_placement = {i: list(placement[i]) for i in range(gpu_num)}
        
        # Decide between move or swap based on temperature and model diversity
        # Higher temperature → more swaps; lower temperature → more moves
        if torch.rand(1).item() < 0.4 + 0.6 * (1.0 - temperature):
            # Move a random model
            non_empty_gpus = [i for i in range(gpu_num) if new_placement[i]]
            if not non_empty_gpus:
                return new_placement
            
            src_gpu = torch.randint(0, len(non_empty_gpus), (1,)).item()
            src_gpu = non_empty_gpus[src_gpu]
            
            model_idx = torch.randint(0, len(new_placement[src_gpu]), (1,)).item()
            model = new_placement[src_gpu].pop(model_idx)
            
            # Try to place on a different GPU
            target_gpus = [i for i in range(gpu_num) if i != src_gpu]
            valid_targets = []
            for gpu_id in target_gpus:
                size = sum(m.model_size for m in new_placement[gpu_id]) + model.model_size
                if size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    valid_targets.append(gpu_id)
            
            if valid_targets:
                # Prefer GPUs with lower current KVPR, but with more randomness at higher temp
                target_scores = []
                for gpu_id in valid_targets:
                    current_size = sum(m.model_size for m in new_placement[gpu_id])
                    current_pressure = sum(m.req_rate / m.slo for m in new_placement[gpu_id])
                    if current_size == 0:
                        target_scores.append(0.0)
                    else:
                        target_scores.append(current_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - current_size))
                
                # Use temperature-dependent entropy: higher temp → more random
                if temperature > 0.2:
                    # Add noise to encourage exploration
                    noise = torch.randn(len(target_scores)) * 0.05
                    scores_with_noise = torch.tensor(target_scores) + noise
                    target_idx = torch.argmin(scores_with_noise).item()
                else:
                    target_idx = torch.argmin(torch.tensor(target_scores)).item()
                
                target_gpu = valid_targets[target_idx]
                new_placement[target_gpu].append(model)
            else:
                # No valid move, put back
                new_placement[src_gpu].append(model)
        
        else:
            # Swap two models
            non_empty_gpus = [i for i in range(gpu_num) if new_placement[i]]
            if len(non_empty_gpus) < 2:
                return new_placement
            
            # Pick two different GPUs
            src_idx = torch.randint(0, len(non_empty_gpus), (1,)).item()
            src_gpu = non_empty_gpus[src_idx]
            
            remaining_gpus = [g for g in non_empty_gpus if g != src_gpu]
            if not remaining_gpus:
                return new_placement
            
            tgt_idx = torch.randint(0, len(remaining_gpus), (1,)).item()
            tgt_gpu = remaining_gpus[tgt_idx]
            
            # Pick models
            src_model_idx = torch.randint(0, len(new_placement[src_gpu]), (1,)).item()
            tgt_model_idx = torch.randint(0, len(new_placement[tgt_gpu]), (1,)).item()
            
            src_model = new_placement[src_gpu][src_model_idx]
            tgt_model = new_placement[tgt_gpu][tgt_model_idx]
            
            # Check if swap is valid
            src_size = sum(m.model_size for m in new_placement[src_gpu]) - src_model.model_size + tgt_model.model_size
            tgt_size = sum(m.model_size for m in new_placement[tgt_gpu]) - tgt_model.model_size + src_model.model_size
            
            if src_size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE and tgt_size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                new_placement[src_gpu][src_model_idx] = tgt_model
                new_placement[tgt_gpu][tgt_model_idx] = src_model
        
        return new_placement
    
    # Step 4: Simulated Annealing
    initial_placement = generate_initial_placement()
    current_placement = initial_placement
    current_score = calculate_max_kvpr(current_placement)
    
    best_placement = {i: list(current_placement[i]) for i in range(gpu_num)}
    best_score = current_score
    
    # Temperature schedule
    initial_temp = 1.2  # Slightly higher initial temp for more exploration
    final_temp = 0.0005  # Lower final temp for finer convergence
    alpha = 0.993  # Slightly slower cooling
    max_iterations = 3000  # More iterations for deeper search
    
    temperature = initial_temp
    
    for iteration in range(max_iterations):
        # Generate neighbor
        neighbor_placement = generate_neighbor(current_placement, temperature)
        neighbor_score = calculate_max_kvpr(neighbor_placement)
        
        # Acceptance criterion
        delta = neighbor_score - current_score
        
        # Accept if better or with probability based on temperature
        if delta < 0 or torch.rand(1).item() < math.exp(-delta / (temperature + 1e-9)):
            current_placement = neighbor_placement
            current_score = neighbor_score
            
            # Update best
            if current_score < best_score:
                best_score = current_score
                best_placement = {i: list(current_placement[i]) for i in range(gpu_num)}
        
        # Cool down
        temperature = max(initial_temp * (alpha ** iteration), final_temp)
        
        # Early termination with patience: if no improvement in last 100 steps and temp low
        if temperature <= final_temp * 1.2 and iteration > 200:
            if iteration % 100 == 0:
                # Check for stagnation
                if iteration > 200 and best_score == current_score:
                    break
    
    # Final validation
    for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
        total_size = sum(m.model_size for m in best_placement[gpu_id])
        if total_size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
            raise ValueError(f"GPU {gpu_id} exceeds memory: {total_size} > {GPU_MEM_SIZE}")
    
    placed_count = sum(len(best_placement[gpu_id]) for gpu_id in range(gpu_num))
    if placed_count != n_models:
        raise ValueError(f"Expected {n_models} models, placed {placed_count}")
    
    return best_placement

from dataclasses import dataclass
import torch
import heapq
from typing import List, Dict

GPU_MEM_SIZE = 80  # GB

@dataclass
class Model:
    model_name: str
    model_size: int   # GB
    req_rate: int     # requests per second
    slo: int          # service level objective (latency target)
    cur_gpu_id: int   # current GPU assignment (can be ignored)


def compute_model_placement(gpu_num: int, models: list[Model]) -> dict[int, list[Model]]:
    '''
    Compute optimal model placement across GPUs to minimize max KVPR.

    Args:
        gpu_num: Number of available GPUs (typically 5-10)
        models: List of Model objects to place

    Returns:
        dict mapping gpu_id (int) to list of Models assigned to that GPU
        Example: {0: [model_a, model_b], 1: [model_c], 2: [model_d, model_e]}

    Constraints:
        - Each model must be assigned to exactly one GPU
        - Total model_size on each GPU must not exceed GPU_MEM_SIZE (80GB)

    Goal:
        Minimize max(KVPR) across all GPUs where:
        KVPR(gpu) = sum(req_rate/slo) / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - sum(model_size))
    '''
    
    # Precompute pressure and size for each model
    model_pressure = [m.req_rate / m.slo for m in models]
    model_size = [m.model_size for m in models]
    n_models = len(models)
    
    # Calculate theoretical lower bound for optimal KVPR
    total_pressure = sum(model_pressure)
    total_size = sum(model_size)
    total_free_memory = gpu_num * GPU_MEM_SIZE - total_size
    
    if total_free_memory <= 0:
        raise ValueError("Total model size exceeds total GPU memory")
    
    min_kvpr_lower_bound = total_pressure / total_free_memory
    
    # NEW APPROACH: Min-Max Bin Packing with Dynamic Programming + Greedy Refinement
    # Instead of binary search, we use a direct Min-Max optimization approach
    
    # Sort models by pressure-to-size ratio (descending) to prioritize high-impact models
    # This is a "Longest Processing Time" variant for this objective
    indexed_models = sorted(
        enumerate(models), 
        key=lambda x: (model_pressure[x[0]] / model_size[x[0]] + 1e-9), 
        reverse=True
    )
    
    # Initialize GPU states
    placement = {gpu_id: [] for gpu_id in range(gpu_num)}
    gpu_sizes = [0] * gpu_num
    gpu_pressures = [0.0] * gpu_num
    
    # Phase 1: Greedy Min-Max Assignment with Adaptive Thresholding
    # Assign each model to the GPU that minimizes the resulting max KVPR
    # Introduce a small bias toward GPUs with lower current KVPR to avoid overloading
    for idx, model in indexed_models:
        size = model_size[idx]
        pressure = model_pressure[idx]
        
        # Calculate potential KVPR for each GPU if we place this model there
        best_gpu = None
        best_max_kvpr = float('inf')
        
        for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                continue
            
            # Calculate what the max KVPR would be if we place here
            new_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] + pressure
            new_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size
            new_kvpr = new_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_size)
            
            # Find the maximum KVPR across ALL GPUs if we place here
            temp_max = new_kvpr
            for other_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if other_gpu == gpu_id:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[other_gpu] > 0:
                    other_kvpr = gpu_pressures[other_gpu] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[other_gpu])
                    temp_max = max(temp_max, other_kvpr)
            
            # Apply a small penalty to GPUs that are already near full capacity
            # This encourages spreading out models more evenly
            capacity_ratio = gpu_sizes[gpu_id] / GPU_MEM_SIZE
            if capacity_ratio > 0.85:  # High utilization penalty
                temp_max *= 1.02
            
            # Minimize the maximum KVPR
            if temp_max < best_max_kvpr:
                best_max_kvpr = temp_max
                best_gpu = gpu_id
        
        # If no GPU can fit, try any available GPU (shouldn't happen with valid inputs)
        if best_gpu is None:
            for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] + size <= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    best_gpu = gpu_id
                    break
        
        if best_gpu is None:
            raise ValueError(f"Cannot place model {model.model_name}")
        
        # Place the model
        placement[best_gpu].append(model)
        gpu_sizes[best_gpu] += size
        gpu_pressures[best_gpu] += pressure
    
    # Phase 2: Local Search with Simulated Annealing (with adaptive cooling)
    # Iteratively improve by swapping models between GPUs
    # Use a more aggressive initial cooling schedule
    
    def calculate_current_max_kvpr():
        max_val = 0.0
        for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] > 0 and gpu_sizes[gpu_id] < GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                kvpr = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[gpu_id])
                max_val = max(max_val, kvpr)
        return max_val
    
    current_max = calculate_current_max_kvpr()
    improved = True
    iterations = 0
    max_iterations = 1500  # Reduced to avoid overfitting to noise
    temperature = 1.0
    cooling_rate = 0.995  # Faster cooling for quicker convergence
    
    while improved and iterations < max_iterations:
        improved = False
        iterations += 1
        
        # Find the GPU with maximum KVPR
        max_gpu = -1
        max_kvpr = -1.0
        for gpu_id in range(gpu_num):
            if gpu_sizes[gpu_id] > 0 and gpu_sizes[gpu_id] < GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                kvpr = gpu_pressures[gpu_id] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[gpu_id])
                if kvpr > max_kvpr:
                    max_kvpr = kvpr
                    max_gpu = gpu_id
        
        if max_gpu == -1:
            break
        
        # Try to move models from max_gpu to other GPUs
        best_move = None
        best_improvement = 0.0
        
        for i, model in enumerate(placement[max_gpu]):
            model_idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressure[model_idx]
            s = model_size[model_idx]
            
            # Calculate the "burden" this model adds to max_gpu
            burden = p / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - (gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s)) if (gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s) < GPU_MEM_SIZE else float('inf')
            
            for target_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                if target_gpu == max_gpu:
                    continue
                if gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s > GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                # Calculate new KVPRs
                new_max_pressure = gpu_pressures[max_gpu] - p
                new_max_size = gpu_sizes[max_gpu] - s
                new_target_pressure = gpu_pressures[target_gpu] + p
                new_target_size = gpu_sizes[target_gpu] + s
                
                if new_max_size <= 0 or new_target_size >= GPU_MEM_SIZE:
                    continue
                
                new_max_kvpr = new_max_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_max_size)
                new_target_kvpr = new_target_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_target_size)
                
                # Find the new overall max KVPR
                new_overall_max = max(new_max_kvpr, new_target_kvpr)
                for other_gpu in range(gpu_num):
                    if other_gpu != max_gpu and other_gpu != target_gpu and gpu_sizes[other_gpu] > 0:
                        other_kvpr = gpu_pressures[other_gpu] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[other_gpu])
                        new_overall_max = max(new_overall_max, other_kvpr)
                
                improvement = max_kvpr - new_overall_max
                if improvement > best_improvement:
                    best_improvement = improvement
                    best_move = (i, max_gpu, target_gpu, model)
        
        # Apply the best move if it improves things or meets temperature threshold
        if best_move and best_improvement > 1e-9:
            i, src_gpu, dst_gpu, model = best_move
            model_idx = models.index(model)
            p = model_pressure[model_idx]
            s = model_size[model_idx]
            
            placement[src_gpu].pop(i)
            placement[dst_gpu].append(model)
            gpu_sizes[src_gpu] -= s
            gpu_sizes[dst_gpu] += s
            gpu_pressures[src_gpu] -= p
            gpu_pressures[dst_gpu] += p
            
            improved = True
            current_max = calculate_current_max_kvpr()
        
        # Cool down
        temperature *= cooling_rate
    
    # Phase 3: Final Optimization - Try swapping pairs of models
    # This can sometimes find better local optima
    # Reduce number of attempts and increase threshold for improvement
    for _ in range(30):  # Fewer attempts to reduce runtime
        best_swap = None
        best_improvement = 0.0
        
        # Try swapping models between any two GPUs
        for gpu_a in range(gpu_num):
            for gpu_b in range(gpu_num):
                if gpu_a >= gpu_b:
                    continue
                if not placement[gpu_a] or not placement[gpu_b]:
                    continue
                
                # Try swapping each model from A with each model from B
                for i, model_a in enumerate(placement[gpu_a]):
                    model_a_idx = models.index(model_a)
                    p_a = model_pressure[model_a_idx]
                    s_a = model_size[model_a_idx]
                    
                    for j, model_b in enumerate(placement[gpu_b]):
                        model_b_idx = models.index(model_b)
                        p_b = model_pressure[model_b_idx]
                        s_b = model_size[model_b_idx]
                        
                        # Check if swap is valid (memory constraints)
                        if (gpu_sizes[gpu_a] - s_a + s_b > GPU_MEM_SIZE or 
                            gpu_sizes[gpu_b] - s_b + s_a > GPU_MEM_SIZE):
                            continue
                        
                        # Calculate new KVPRs
                        new_a_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_a] - p_a + p_b
                        new_a_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_a] - s_a + s_b
                        new_b_pressure = gpu_pressures[gpu_b] - p_b + p_a
                        new_b_size = gpu_sizes[gpu_b] - s_b + s_a
                        
                        if new_a_size <= 0 or new_b_size <= 0:
                            continue
                        
                        new_a_kvpr = new_a_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_a_size)
                        new_b_kvpr = new_b_pressure / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - new_b_size)
                        
                        # Calculate current max
                        current_max = 0.0
                        for g in range(gpu_num):
                            if g == gpu_a:
                                current_max = max(current_max, new_a_kvpr)
                            elif g == gpu_b:
                                current_max = max(current_max, new_b_kvpr)
                            elif gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                                current_max = max(current_max, gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g]))
                        
                        # Compare with old max
                        old_max = 0.0
                        for g in range(gpu_num):
                            if gpu_sizes[g] > 0:
                                old_max = max(old_max, gpu_pressures[g] / (GPU_MEM_SIZE - gpu_sizes[g]))
                        
                        improvement = old_max - current_max
                        if improvement > best_improvement:
                            best_improvement = improvement
                            best_swap = (gpu_a, i, gpu_b, j)
        
        if best_swap and best_improvement > 1e-8:  # Slightly looser threshold
            gpu_a, i, gpu_b, j = best_swap
            model_a = placement[gpu_a][i]
            model_b = placement[gpu_b][j]
            
            model_a_idx = models.index(model_a)
            model_b_idx = models.index(model_b)
            p_a = model_pressure[model_a_idx]
            s_a = model_size[model_a_idx]
            p_b = model_pressure[model_b_idx]
            s_b = model_size[model_b_idx]
            
            # Perform swap
            placement[gpu_a][i] = model_b
            placement[gpu_b][j] = model_a
